title: The deleted subjects.test.ts (CR-650, CR-651) was the only test verifying that resolveSubjectName and classifySubject throwing UpstreamLlmError propagates through the global onError handler (index.ts)
body_excerpt: The deleted subjects.test.ts (CR-650, CR-651) was the only test verifying that resolveSubjectName and classifySubject throwing UpstreamLlmError propagates through the global onError handler (index.ts) to 502
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/subjects.test.ts exists with tests for /v1/subjects/resolve and /v1/subjects/classify, but the test app uses a local `app.onError((err, c) => c.json({ code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR', message: err.message }, 500))` handler (line 119-121), not the real global handler in index.ts. No test asserts 502 + LLM_UNAVAILABLE for UpstreamLlmError on resolve/classify. Grep for UpstreamLlm in subjects.test.ts returns 0 results.
suggested_notion_title: Missing 502 test: UpstreamLlmError from resolveSubjectName/classifySubject not covered through real global onError handler
suggested_notion_body: subjects.test.ts mounts a local minimal onError that returns 500 for all errors. The real global handler in index.ts maps UpstreamLlmError → 502 LLM_UNAVAILABLE + Sentry. No test exercises this path for /v1/subjects/resolve or /v1/subjects/classify. Need at least one test using the real index.ts app (or integration) that triggers UpstreamLlmError on these routes and asserts 502.

title: A prior review flagged that the instanceof SubjectNotLanguageLearningError break test was missing. That finding is resolved by this PR: subjects-language-setup.test.ts has the negative-path case as the second test
body_excerpt: A prior review flagged that the instanceof SubjectNotLanguageLearningError break test was missing. That finding is resolved by this PR: subjects-language-setup.test.ts has the negative-path case as the secon
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/subjects-language-setup.test.ts lines 76-88: test 'does not classify a generic same-message error as validation failure' throws a plain Error with identical message and asserts 500 (not 422). This confirms instanceof check is real, not string-matching. Finding was already marked as resolved by the CCR.

title: [HIGH] `isAppHelpQuery` regex: first branch is too broad**
body_excerpt: [HIGH] `isAppHelpQuery` regex: first branch is too broad** The `APP_HELP_FRAMED` regex's opening alternative is `where (do i|are my|can i|is the) (find|see|change|access|get to)\b` with no object constra
evidence: apps/api/src/services/app-help-map.ts line 50: APP_HELP_FRAMED now constrains the object to a specific noun list: (notes|saved|saved replies|saved explanations|settings|preferences|bookmarks?|notifications?|profile|account|mode|library|app|sessions?|conversations?). Fix committed in 5b284a96b 'fix(api): narrow app-help review guards'. 'where do i find the quadratic formula?' no longer matches.

title: [MEDIUM] Idempotency key format change breaks Resend deduplication**
body_excerpt: [MEDIUM] Idempotency key format change breaks Resend deduplication** `buildEmailIdempotencyKey('weekly', parentId, reportWeek)` emits `value(weekly):value(<uuid>):value(2026-05-12)`. The old template liter
evidence: apps/api/src/services/dedupe-key.ts lines 33-38: buildLegacyEmailIdempotencyKey is introduced with @deprecated tag, preserving the old 'weekly-uuid-date' format. weekly-progress-push.ts line 718 and monthly-report-cron.ts line 475 both call buildLegacyEmailIdempotencyKey for existing email types, preserving Resend dedup continuity. New email types (consent-reminder, feedback-delivery-failed) use the new buildEmailIdempotencyKey.

title: The `.test-receipts/mobile.json` receipt was generated with a 5-file `--testMatch` run, not with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile`
body_excerpt: The `.test-receipts/mobile.json` receipt was generated with a 5-file `--testMatch` run, not with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` (which runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test`). CLAUDE.md is expl
evidence: .test-receipts/ directory does not exist in the current working tree (PowerShell Test-Path returns False). The receipt file in question no longer exists — the directory was removed. scripts/record-test-receipt.sh also does not exist in scripts/. The finding was specific to a PR artifact that is no longer present on main.

title: setVoiceEnabled(true)` schedules a React state update that has not been flushed when `await handleVoicePress()` immediately follows.
body_excerpt: `setVoiceEnabled(true)` schedules a React state update that has not been flushed when `await handleVoicePress()` immediately follows. `handleVoicePress` does not gate on `isVoiceEnabled` (recording starts
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/session/ChatShell.tsx lines 484-503: handleEnableVoiceAndRecord now uses a pendingVoiceStartRef pattern. When !isVoiceEnabled, it sets pendingVoiceStartRef.current=true and returns; a useEffect at lines 499-503 fires after isVoiceEnabled has updated to true and then calls handleVoicePress(). The React state flush race is resolved.

title: The block comment still says "Long-press flips voice ON and starts recording" — the old design. Both `onPress` and `onLongPress` now call `handleEnableVoiceAndRecord`.
body_excerpt: The block comment still says "Long-press flips voice ON and starts recording" — the old design. Both `onPress` and `onLongPress` now call `handleEnableVoiceAndRecord`. If intentional, remove `onLongPress`
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/session/ChatShell.tsx: grep for 'onLongPress' returns no matches. The 'Long-press flips voice ON and starts recording' comment is also absent. The stale comment and duplicate onLongPress handler have been removed.

title: When shouldRefundAfterHandler is true, safeRefundQuota is called and the middleware returns early
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts` Line: ~L453 Issue: When `shouldRefundAfterHandler` is true, `safeRefundQuota` is called and the middleware returns early — skipping the KV cache update block.
evidence: apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts lines 524-530: after safeRefundQuota, the code returns immediately without updating KV. The subsequent KV update block (lines 532-563) is never reached on the refund path. Commit 0aa08d20f added withoutQuotaHeaders() to strip headers from the error response but did NOT add KV invalidation after refund. Stale KV will continue to show decremented count until TTL expires.
suggested_notion_title: Stale KV quota cache after safeRefundQuota: user sees false QUOTA_EXCEEDED on next valid request
suggested_notion_body: metering.ts: when shouldRefundAfterHandler is true, safeRefundQuota restores the DB quota but the KV cache still shows the decremented count. A user at quota limit who hits a bad-request error will see false QUOTA_EXCEEDED on their next valid request until KV TTL expires. Fix: after safeRefundQuota, write the restored count to KV (or invalidate the KV entry).

title: After safeRefundQuota the middleware returns early, skipping the writeSubscriptionStatus KV update.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts` Line: L464–L480 Issue: After `safeRefundQuota` the middleware `return`s early, skipping the `writeSubscriptionStatus` KV update. The KV will continue to show t
evidence: Same root cause as PR #281 comment-4460050469 (duplicate finding from a different reviewer). apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts lines 524-530: return after safeRefundQuota skips KV update. This is the same unaddressed pattern verified above.

title: maybeReplayIdempotentSessionRequest returns an inline anonymous response shape { replayed, clientId, status, assistantTurnReady, latestExchangeId }.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts` Line: L195–L214 Issue: `maybeReplayIdempotentSessionRequest` returns an inline anonymous response shape `{ replayed, clientId, status, assistantTurnReady, late
evidence: apps/api/src/middleware/metering.ts lines 237-243: c.json({ replayed: true, clientId: key, status: 'persisted', assistantTurnReady: state.assistantTurnReady, latestExchangeId: state.latestExchangeId }) is still an inline anonymous shape. Grep for idempotencyReplay, IdempotencyReplay, replayed.*clientId in packages/ returns no results — the type is not exported from @eduagent/schemas.
suggested_notion_title: Idempotency replay response shape not in @eduagent/schemas — breaks mobile type safety
suggested_notion_body: metering.ts maybeReplayIdempotentSessionRequest emits { replayed, clientId, status, assistantTurnReady, latestExchangeId } as an inline anonymous type. Per CLAUDE.md, all API-facing types must be defined in @eduagent/schemas. Mobile client has no typed contract for this response. Add idempotencyReplayResponseSchema to @eduagent/schemas and use it here.

title: tint.solid + '33' appends a hex-alpha suffix via string concatenation.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/mobile/src/components/home/ParentHomeScreen.tsx` Line: ~1002 Issue: `tint.solid + '33'` appends a hex-alpha suffix via string concatenation. `SubjectTint.solid` has no compile-time guarantee
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/home/ParentHomeScreen.tsx line 460: `borderColor: tint ? tint.solid + '26' : colors.primary + '26'` — the hex alpha suffix is still concatenated directly (value changed from '33' to '26' but the unsafe pattern remains). The withOpacity() utility exists at apps/mobile/src/lib/color-opacity.ts and is used in TopicStatusRow, TopicHeader, etc., but is NOT imported or used in ParentHomeScreen.tsx for this value.
suggested_notion_title: ParentHomeScreen ConversationStarterCard: tint.solid + '26' unsafe hex-alpha concatenation not replaced with withOpacity()
suggested_notion_body: ConversationStarterCard in ParentHomeScreen.tsx still uses tint.solid + '26' (raw string concat). withOpacity() utility exists and handles 3-digit hex, rgb(), hsl() formats. Replace with withOpacity(tint.solid, 0.149) — identical to 26/FF ≈ 14.9% alpha. Same fix needed for colors.primary + '26' fallback.

title: The new style={({ pressed }) => ({...})} function fully replaces the old Platform.OS === 'web' ? { cursor: 'pointer' } : undefined prop.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/mobile/src/components/home/ParentHomeScreen.tsx` Line: ~997 Issue: The new `style={({ pressed }) => ({...})}` function fully replaces the old `Platform.OS === 'web' ? { cursor: 'pointer' }
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/home/ParentHomeScreen.tsx: grep for 'onLongPress' and 'style={({ pressed })' returns no matches. Multiple Pressable elements still have explicit style={Platform.OS === 'web' ? { cursor: 'pointer' } : undefined} at lines 536, 590, 747, 1017, 1097. The pressed-function style pattern that would have dropped cursor:pointer is no longer present.

title: '33' is a hardcoded hex-alpha suffix concatenated onto tint.solid.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/mobile/src/components/home/ParentHomeScreen.tsx` Line: `borderColor: tint ? tint.solid + '33' : ...` Issue: `'33'` is a hardcoded hex-alpha suffix concatenated onto `tint.solid`. This silent
evidence: Duplicate of comment-4462620845 tint.solid finding (different CCR reviewer). apps/mobile/src/components/home/ParentHomeScreen.tsx line 460: still uses tint.solid + '26' (alpha changed from '33' but unsafe raw string concat remains). withOpacity() utility at apps/mobile/src/lib/color-opacity.ts is not applied here.

title: practiceSummaries.values().next().value picks whichever profile happens to be first in the Map iterator and assigns it as the shared emptyResult.practiceSummary.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/services/progress.ts` Line: `getOverallProgressBatch` early-exit Issue: `practiceSummaries.values().next().value` picks whichever profile happens to be first in the Map iterator and a
evidence: apps/api/src/services/progress.ts lines 631-668: emptyResult.practiceSummary is still set via practiceSummaries.values().next().value (line 636). When a profile in a batch has no subjects AND no practice summary entry, it receives the shared emptyResult object which carries another profile's practiceSummary data — cross-profile data leak. No subsequent fix commits found for progress.ts after bf5e360c5.
suggested_notion_title: Critical: getOverallProgressBatch cross-profile data leak — emptyResult.practiceSummary set from first Map iterator value
suggested_notion_body: progress.ts getOverallProgressBatch: when allSubjects.length===0, emptyResult.practiceSummary is set once from practiceSummaries.values().next().value. For a batch with ≥2 profiles where one has a practice summary and another doesn't, the profile without a summary receives the other profile's practiceSummary. Fix: replace the shared emptyResult pattern — build per-profile empty results inline in the loop, using practiceSummaries.get(pid) with an explicit zero-fallback.

title: captureExceptionSpy.mockRestore() is called before the assertion that checks whether captureException was invoked.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.integration.test.ts` Line: `captureExceptionSpy` assertion block Issue: `captureExceptionSpy.mockRestore()` is called **before** the assertion that
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.integration.test.ts lines 1421-1428: expect(captureExceptionSpy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(...) at line 1421 comes BEFORE captureExceptionSpy.mockRestore() at line 1428. Assertion order is correct.

title: The advancedLlmProvider parameter is wired through prepareExchangeContext() options and tested by calling the pure routing function directly, but there is no wiring from the route handler
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts` Line: `resolveExchangeLlmRouting` Issue: The `advancedLlmProvider` parameter is wired through `prepareExchangeContext()` options and tested by ca
evidence: apps/api/src/routes/sessions.ts: grep for 'advancedLlmProvider' returns no matches. The parameter is present in session-exchange.ts (lines 147, 765, 985, 1298) and tested in session-exchange.test.ts, but the sessions route handler never reads or passes it. The feature is dead in production — advancedLlmProvider will always be undefined.
suggested_notion_title: advancedLlmProvider dead in production: sessions route handler does not wire the parameter
suggested_notion_body: session-exchange.ts resolveExchangeLlmRouting accepts advancedLlmProvider but sessions.ts route handler never reads or forwards it. The unit tests call the pure function directly (bypassing the route). In production the value is always undefined. Fix: determine the intended source for advancedLlmProvider (request body, header, subscription meta) and wire it through the route handler.

title: The lock window uses [card.updatedAt.getTime() - 1, card.updatedAt.getTime() + 1).
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/services/retention-data.ts` Line: `updateRetentionFromSession` optimistic lock Issue: The lock window uses `[card.updatedAt.getTime() - 1, card.updatedAt.getTime() + 1)`. The lower bo
evidence: apps/api/src/services/retention-data.ts lines 1349-1350: updatedAtLockWindowStart = card.updatedAt.getTime() - 1 and updatedAtLockWindowEnd = card.updatedAt.getTime() + 1 are unchanged. The -1ms lower bound unnecessarily widens the lock window to accept timestamps 1ms earlier than the read value. No fix commits since the PR.
suggested_notion_title: Retention optimistic lock window too wide: lower bound -1ms accepts stale DB timestamps
suggested_notion_body: retention-data.ts updateRetentionFromSession: lock window is [updatedAt-1ms, updatedAt+1ms]. The -1ms lower bound allows concurrent writes that set updatedAt 1ms earlier than the read value to slip through the lock. The +1ms upper bound is justified for JS/Postgres microsecond rounding but the lower bound should be exact (updatedAt). Fix: set updatedAtLockWindowStart = new Date(card.updatedAt.getTime()).

title: The batch query fetches all historical snapshots for all profile IDs with no date-range filter.
body_excerpt: File: `apps/api/src/services/dashboard.ts` Line: `buildChildProgressSummariesBatch` Issue: The batch query fetches all historical snapshots for all profile IDs with no date-range filter. The previous `ge
evidence: apps/api/src/services/dashboard.ts lines 513-517: db.query.progressSnapshots.findMany({ where: inArray(progressSnapshots.profileId, profileIds), orderBy: desc(progressSnapshots.snapshotDate) }) — no date-range filter. For children with months of daily snapshots, this returns unbounded rows and deduplicates in JS (lines 547-566). No date-bounding fix applied since bf5e360c5.
suggested_notion_title: Unbounded snapshot query in buildChildProgressSummariesBatch: no date-range filter, OOM risk for long-running children
suggested_notion_body: dashboard.ts buildChildProgressSummariesBatch fetches ALL historical snapshots for all profile IDs. Only the latest + previous-week snapshot per child are actually used. Fix: add a WHERE snapshotDate >= (today - 14 days) or equivalent, or fetch only the 2 most recent snapshots per profile using a window function / LIMIT subquery. The previous getLatestSnapshotOnOrBefore used LIMIT 1.
